body.title: CLOSED: Migrate cortex.py 129 SQLite sites to native Postgres
body.result: cortex.py fully migrated: INSERT OR REPLACE→ON CONFLICT, lastrowid→RETURNING, dead SQLite branches removed, 129 ?→%s. 4189 tests pass. commit bc7f65f6
body.description:
cortex.py has 129 SQL lines with `?` placeholders plus several INSERT OR REPLACE/IGNORE patterns and two lastrowid calls. Load-bearing file (5000+ lines) — HIGH-INERTIA, requires Akien pre-approval before sprinting.

Migration scope:
- Lines 1508/1514: `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO memories … VALUES (?,×22)` → `ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET …` (22 columns), `%s`
- Lines 1637/1642: same pattern for executemany
- Lines 1319/1377/1400: `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO _migrations` → `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, `%s`
- Lines 3932–3947: `_upsert_embedding` already has PGDatabaseProxy ON CONFLICT branch + SQLite fallback → remove else (SQLite) branch
- Line 4550: `obs_id = cur.lastrowid` (twm_observations integer SERIAL) → add `RETURNING id`; `obs_id = cur.fetchone()[0]`
- Line 5400: `return cur.lastrowid` (interpretive_edges integer SERIAL) → add `RETURNING id`; `return cur.fetchone()[0]`
- Line 5697: `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO lists … VALUES (?,×7)` → `ON CONFLICT (list_name, item_key, instance_id) DO UPDATE SET …`, `%s`
- All other `?` lines → mechanical `%s` sweep

**json_extract investigation required:** Lines 1974/1977/2076 use `json_extract(metadata, ?)` — SQLite JSON function with NO db_proxy translation. These paths are likely broken on Postgres today or dead code. Must grep call sites and test before changing. If dead: remove. If live: replace with JSONB operators.

**Rollback:** `git revert <hash>` restores previous behavior because cortex.py is the sole memory-store writer; a revert fully undoes the SQL changes.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py`
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database; theigors/rules/safeguards (HIGH-inertia file)
**Scope boundary:** cortex.py only; no shim changes; json_extract investigation is part of this ticket.
**Test plan:** Run full test suite (4164+ tests); grep confirms no `INSERT OR REPLACE`, `INSERT OR IGNORE`, `lastrowid`, `json_extract` in cortex.py after migration.
**Pass condition:** All tests green; Igor session processes memories without error; `grep -n 'INSERT OR REPLACE\|INSERT OR IGNORE\|lastrowid\|json_extract' wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py` returns 0 results.

Validation: Igor memory store accepts inserts; twm_observations and interpretive_edges inserts return correct integer ids in logs.
Cleanup: test fixtures via conftest.pg_test_schema.
Observable via: log.INFO("memory stored id=...") at cortex.py insert paths.
